episodes.title: Gloria Allred on Epstein Ties & Mountbatten-Windsor Arrest | Laura Coates Live CNN Interview
episodes.description:
Gloria Allred discusses the arrest of Andrew Mountbatten-Windsor during an appearance on CNN’s Laura Coates Live.

In this interview, famed victims’ rights attorney Gloria Allred discusses Mr. Mountbatten-Windsor’s arrest on suspicion of misconduct in public office and revisits her long-standing public calls for him to speak with law enforcement regarding his alleged involvement with Jeffrey Epstein. Ms. Allred notes that despite evidence of alleged child sex trafficking, no arrests have been made on those charges. She raises concerns about accountability and asks, “Where is the priority for crimes against women and children?” emphasizing the need for transparency and justice for survivors.

🔗 Learn more about Gloria Allred’s advocacy for survivors:

episodes.title: BBC News Breaking | Gloria Allred Discusses Insights on the Release of the Epstein Files
episodes.description:
BBC “BBC News Breaking.” Gloria Allred, victims’ rights attorney who represents numerous survivors of Jeffrey Epstein’s abuse, joins BBC News to discuss the latest release of over 3 million documents related to Jeffrey Epstein. Many of the documents, which were released on Friday, January 30, 2026, revealed personal identifying information of victims, including contact information, photo evidence, and unredacted names of new victims who had not been previously named before.  Ms. Allred claims that the recent release of Epstein files was at best “incompetent.” “Now it seems that the sexual predators are being protected,” Ms. Allred says, “…not the survivors – who are being shamed, humiliated…. Once again, they have been denied justice.”

episodes.title: Isa Soares Tonight on CNN | Gloria Allred Provides Insights on the Release of the Epstein Files
episodes.description:
CNN “Isa Soares Tonight.” Gloria Allred, a victims’ rights attorney who represents more than two dozen survivors of Jeffrey Epstein, joins Isa Soares on CNN to discuss the latest release of the Epstein files on Friday. The latest drop of Jeffrey Epstein related documents contained unredacted information of the victims, which included photos, names, contact information, texts, and drivers’ licenses.  Ms. Allred claims that the victims and the attorneys who represent them are “horrified” by the handling of these documents as they were promised that any identifying information would be redacted. Additionally, according to Ms. Allred, the Deputy Attorney General Todd Blanche stated that there would be no new releases of documents and no further prosecutions of any individuals involved. “This is so devastating to them,” Ms. Allred said about the victims. “It’s such an invasion of privacy, and they can’t trust the Department of Justice anymore,” she added.

episodes.title: Press conference today to respond to the upcoming House vote re: the release of the Epstein files
episodes.description: Attorney Gloria Allred held a press conference today with her client, Alicia Arden.  Alicia reacted to President Trump’s decision yesterday to tell Republicans in the House to vote to release the Jeffrey Epstein files in the possession of the Justice Department. Ms. Allred commented on President Trump’s decision to direct the Department of Justice (DOJ) to investigate high-level Democrats who associated with Jeffrey Epstein and the impact that may have on the vote this week and on the survivors of Jeffrey Epstein and Ghislaine Maxwell.  Ms. Arden is the first survivor of Jeffrey Epstein to have proof that she filed a police report with sexual allegations against him.

episodes.title: August 6, 2025 Gloria Allred Press Conference with her client, an early victim of Jeffrey Epstein
episodes.description: Today a press conference was held at the Los Angeles office of attorney Gloria Allred with her client, Alicia Arden, who was the first victim to have documented proof that she filed a police report against Jeffrey Epstein in 1997 in Santa Monica.  Ms. Arden’s police report alleged that Jeffrey Epstein committed a sexual battery against her when she met him to audition as a model to appear in the Victoria’s Secret catalogue.

episodes.title: Gloria Allred and an alleged victim of Sean Diddy Combs file a lawsuit today against Combs
episodes.description: Attorney Gloria Allred held a press conference today with her client, an alleged victim of Sean Diddy Combs. They discuss in the conference a lawsuit which was filed today against Combs.

episodes.title: Press Conference with Gloria Allred and UK legal team featured in ‘Al-Fayed: Predator at Harrods’
episodes.description: A press conference was held September 20, 2024, in London with the UK legal team featured in ‘Al-Fayed: Predator at Harrods’ and Gloria Allred, the world’s leading women’s and victims’ rights attorney, to discuss their involvement in the investigation and the legal claim against Harrods for failing to provide a safe system of work for their employees.

episodes.title: Gloria Allred Represents Jeffery Esptein Victim that she Reported to Police and Wasn't Investigated.
episodes.description:
Today we are here on the one-year anniversary of the arrest of Jeffrey Epstein.  Mr. Epstein is alleged to have sexually abused hundreds of girls and young women dating back to 1994 or earlier and perhaps continuing until his arrest on July 6, 2019. In fact, Ghislaine Maxwell was just indicted last week on allegations that, “from at least in or about 1994, up to and including at least in or about 1997, Maxwell assisted, facilitated, and contributed to Jeffrey Epstein’s abuse of minor girls…”.
 
 In 1997, my client Alicia Arden, a model, was sexually assaulted and battered by Jeffrey Epstein at the Shutters on the Beach Hotel in Santa Monica. She filed a police report with the Santa Monica Police Department but it appears that little or no investigation was ever done, because after the police report was filed, Ms. Arden was never contacted by the police or by any prosecutor.


 Had Jeffrey Epstein been investigated and had he been prosecuted and convicted, perhaps countless victims would have been spared from the sexual assaults and rapes that they were forced to suffer. 


 Today, I am delivering a letter to the LA County District Attorney and the Santa Monica City Attorney, demanding an investigation into why Ms. Arden’s report to the police in 1997 appears to have been ignored. It took courage for Alicia to file the report, but then it appears that law enforcement failed to pursue it.


 Alicia may be the first victim of Jeffrey Epstein who has written evidence that she did file a police report alleging sexual abuse by Epstein in the 1990’s.


 The failure of law enforcement to follow up on her report is inexcusable, and Alicia deserves answers. The women and girls who were also victimized as a result of the failure of law enforcement to do their job also deserve to know the truth about why Jeffrey Epstein was allowed to victimize Alicia without suffering any legal consequences. Was there a failure to investigate Jeffrey Epstein because he was rich, powerful, and well-connected or were there other reasons? Was the failure to do any meaningful investigation because of rape myths, such as the common myth that if a woman goes to a man’s hotel room, she must have wanted to be sexually assaulted?  Alicia and the public have a right to know. We look forward to the response of law enforcement.

episodes.title: Gloria Allred Files a Lawsuit on Behalf of her Client Teala Davies Against Epstein's Estate
episodes.description:
Statement of Gloria Allred

Today we filed a lawsuit in the United States District Court for the Southern District of New York for Teala Davies.


When Teala Davies was just seventeen years old, living on her own and working full-time to support herself, Jeffrey Epstein preyed upon her, manipulated her to be completely dependent on him, and sexually abused her. 

By age seventeen, Teala had already lived through tremendously challenging circumstances. At eleven years old, she was homeless for a year. At fifteen, Teala began working in order to support herself. 

Epstein was presented to Teala as someone who had learned about her challenging circumstances and had the resources and interest to assist her — including by helping her to attend college and study abroad to see the world. 
She had no idea that he would exploit her and abuse her repeatedly. Ultimately, he raped and sexually assaulted her frequently in a variety of places and settings, including by trafficking Teala to his homes in New York, New Mexico, Florida, the Virgin Islands, and France. He treated her like an object for his sexual gratification.
While it was happening, like so many childhood sex abuse victims, she did not comprehend how it had undone her life, or how it impacted her. She is still just beginning to understand the impact of his pattern of manipulation, sexual assault, and betrayal of her trust. 
We filed this lawsuit today alleging battery, assault, and intentional infliction of emotional distress.
We are seeking compensatory and punitive damages in an amount to be determined at trial.We want justice for Teala. We look forward to winning our case in a court of law.

Statement of Teala Davies

Thank you for coming today.  My name is Teala Davies.

There are so many people in this world, so many stories.  You look at the people around you and it’s easy to forget that everyone has a lifetime of memories they walk with.  You never know who you are sitting next to, what they have been through, or what they might be going through.  I am only one of so many silenced victims out there: past, present, and future victims — victims that feel they have no voice and that are trying to ignore and suppress their horrific memories and their horrific truths for fear of not being believed or heard.

I am here and I am ready to be heard.  I am here to set an example and inspire all victims of sexual abuse to conquer their fear and tell someone.  It’s not only cleansing but you WILL be heard and people WILL listen.  I am here for them.

I was only 17.  I was the perfect victim.  I was on my own and I needed help. Jeffrey Epstein preyed upon me.  He put me in a vulnerable and dependent situation, and took advantage of me.  I was only 17 years old.  I was a little girl.  It took me a long time to break free from his mind control and abuse.  It wasn’t a clean break either.  I still have flashbacks.  It still hurts.  And like I said, I am still 


scared. I am healing from it every day. This complaint that I filed today is a part of that healing.

As we all know, Jeffrey Epstein will never serve the time he needed to serve — time that he could have used to reflect, realize, and regret what he has done to so many innocent young girls. I would have liked him to realize that his actions still affect and haunt his victims to this day and beyond this day.  For many of his victims, he has disabled their ability to receive intimacy and he has disabled their ability to receive love.  For some he has disabled their ability to trust.  Predators like Jeffrey Epstein have such a negative effect on so many innocent girls’ and boys’ lives.  For me and some of the other victims, we have to carry these terrible memories for the rest of our lives, knowing Jeffrey Epstein will never think about us again, and will never suffer the consequences of his crimes against us.

episodes.title: Gloria Allred Files Lawsuit Against Jeffrey Epstein's Estate On Behalf of "Jane Doe 15."
episodes.description:
Today we filed a lawsuit in New York on behalf of “Jane Doe 15,” who was only 15 years old when Jeffrey Epstein preyed upon her, trafficked her and sexually abused her.  

Jane Doe 15 is clearly a victim of child sexual abuse.  She does not wish to disclose her true name.  Nevertheless, she wishes to speak out about what she suffered as a result of meeting Jeffrey Epstein and those who were employed by him or were associated with him.  The law allows her to file a lawsuit as “Jane Doe” and she has chosen to exercise that option.

Although she wishes her legal identity to be protected, she has chosen to let her voice be heard today because she wants to speak her truth and encourage other underage victims of Jeffrey Epstein who have not yet asserted their legal rights to come forward and pursue either a civil lawsuit and/or speak to law enforcement about what they have suffered if they have not already done so.  

Even though Jeffrey Epstein is deceased, we are still permitted to file her case against his estate and we have filed that case this morning in New York.  Her lawsuit alleges battery, assault, and intentional infliction of emotional distress.  She is seeking compensatory damages and punitive damages according to proof at trial.

Jane Doe 15 has also met with law enforcement because she understands that despite Mr. Epstein’s death, there is still an ongoing criminal investigation by the United States Attorneys’ office for the Southern District of New York.

Jane Doe 15 wants to be sure that she has done everything within her power to provide information that she has to assist in this important investigation into those who may have knowingly assisted and conspired to sexually traffic underage girls.  

Although the justice system has clearly failed Epstein victims and survivors in the past, a measure of justice may still be possible for the survivors, both in the criminal and in the civil justice systems.

We are very proud to represent Jane Doe 15 along with our co-counsel, Mariann Wang and Daniel Mullkoff of the law firm of Cuti Hecker Wang LLP in New York.  Jane Doe 15 has chosen to be brave and speak her truth today in order to help others.  She also courageously provided a Victim Impact statement in Federal Court in August 2019 when the Federal judge invited victims to provide their Victim Impact statement after Jeffrey Epstein’s death.  

We will do everything possible to support and protect our client and fight for the justice that she deserves in a New York court of law.
